blurb: A spineless, flattened green dome divided into neat segments and flecked all over with tufts of white — a living sand dollar. Woolly areoles run down each groove, and it pops a yellow flower from the fuzzy crown.

care.light: Bright light with sun, but ease it in — the spineless body can scorch if rushed into intense summer glare. Good light keeps the flat button shape.
care.water: Soak-and-dry and sparing: a thorough drink, then bone dry before the next. Keep nearly dry in winter.
care.soil: A very gritty, mostly-mineral cactus mix; it's rot-prone in anything that stays damp.
care.temperature: Room temperature; keep above ~35°F and dry when cool.
care.dormancy: A summer grower; rest cool and dry over winter to set up spring flowers.
care.feeding: A dilute low-nitrogen cactus feed once or twice in summer.
care.potting: Slow-growing and happy snug; repot infrequently and keep the mix low around the body.
care.propagation: Almost always from seed — it rarely offsets.
care.bloom: A big yellow flower with a red-orange throat from the woolly crown, even on small plants.
care.troubles: Overwatering rot is the main killer; scorch on the exposed body; mealybugs and root mealybugs.
care.toxicity: Non-toxic and, being spineless, unusually safe to handle for a cactus.

sources.title: Astrophytum (Star Cactus) — NC State Extension Gardener Plant Toolbox

shots.cap: A fuzzy green bud rising from the woolly crown.
First bloom — pale-yellow flower with a red-orange throat, days after coming home.
